title: We Need to Talk About Bad Writing
description:
Three years ago I broke my brain. Or, I should say, my brain was broken by grief. That summer my graduate mentor, the writer Aurelie Sheehan, died after a swift and truncated battle with terminal brain cancer. I first learned of Aurelie’s illness in June of 2023, but as I understand it, she received her […]

The post We Need to Talk About Bad Writing appeared first on
hook: A writer's brain broke under grief, and she learned that bad writing is just what happens when you're too raw to perform competence.

title: Who Are You Stacey Levine? What Happens When a “Deeply Weird,” (Very) Small Press Novel is a Pulitzer Finalist
description: It was like any Monday morning at Seattle Central College, the community college where Stacey Levine has taught creative writing for fifteen years. Arriving at work, she sat down at her office desk—a bulletin board with lightly crumpled world maps
hook: A community college writing teacher's 'deeply weird' novel from a tiny press just became a Pulitzer finalist—how did nobody notice her until now?

title: In “Mutual Analysis” with Wallace Shawn’s Moth Days
description: "I do say to myself every day, Well, these crimes that have been committed in order for me to have this lovely fruit salad are inexcusable, but shouldn’t I at least enjoy the fruit salad?"
hook: Wallace Shawn on the guilt of enjoying fruit salad while crimes fund your comfort—philosophy as dinner table conversation.

title: All of a Sudden – first-look review
description: This intimate epic from Japanese filmmaker Ryusuke Hamaguchi expounds on the philosophy of caregiving with creativity, lyricism and love.
hook: Ryusuke Hamaguchi turns caregiving into lyric poetry, turning the mundane weight of staying present into a philosophical act.

title: The Crime Lady: A Few Questions for Emma Garman
description:

hook: Emma Garman investigates how strangers' kindness becomes a legal liability in a culture obsessed with self-sufficiency.

title: Silvesterchlausen
description:
‘We’re not sure what it means or how it started’ – the enigmatic ritual that has existed in Switzerland for centuries

- by Aeon Video
hook: A Swiss ritual nobody outside the region knows exists—masked, costumed figures enacting tradition through surreal performance on New Year's Eve.

title: Beneath the Paving Stones, the River
hook: Why plotlessness matters politically: a manifesto against narrative closure disguised as freedom.
